actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. The Narrator (heightened, fluent). Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> ON CAMERA lip-syncs "-as I say, the house is quiet — always quiet-". prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O. Match appearance of reference <IMAGE_1> exactly.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok dialogue. Character_The_Narrator ON CAMERA lip-syncs exactly: "-as I say, the house is quiet — always quiet-". Start speaking immediately with the first word of the line — do not skip the opening. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Other mouths closed. Speech intelligible; never silent. Secondary layer = soft room tone / Foley. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CONTEXT (prior clip in scene — new cast plate refs attached; match location/lighting if still valid; identity from CHARACTER VARIABLES + locked plates only):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. also on screen: Character_OfficerHale, Character_OfficerBriggs, Character_Officer_Reynolds. He talks on-too freely. The officers smile, polite. The sound clarifies: low, dull, quick-a watch in cotton. From under the floorboards. From under his chair. THE NARRATOR gasps for breath. The officers do not react. They did not hear. Character_The_Narrator still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Medium shot tilts from polite smiling officers down to floorboards under Narrator's chair then back to his gasp. Performance: Acting intensity 8/10: Gasping open mouth and bulging eyes talking too freely Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219 film stock, deep charcoal shadows, sickly cool-blue undertones, dim warm amber highlights
